summary: Promoted ADR 0319 and ADR 0320 into canonical mainline truth as repository version `0.177.142` on top of the already-published `0.177.141` mainline, preserving the verified exact-main `runtime-ai-pool` replay and re-verifying that the dedicated `runtime-ai-lv3` pool keeps Nomad, Traefik, and Dapr healthy while Apache Tika, Gotenberg, and Tesseract OCR remain retired from `docker-runtime-lv3` and the authenticated shared `/v1/gotenberg` gateway path stays healthy.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pyyaml python3 scripts/release_manager.py status --json` recorded repository version `0.177.140` and platform version `0.130.88` as the latest mainline baseline before the initial exact-main cut. The first `--bump patch --released-on 2026-04-02` write updated the protected release surfaces to repository version `0.177.141`; the only failure was the post-write Outline sync to `wiki.lv3.org`, which returned `502 Bad Gateway` after the local repo surfaces had already been written. After ADR 0316 occupied `0.177.141` on `origin/main`, the final merge-to-main integration promoted this same verified runtime-ai rollout as repository version `0.177.142` without replaying the platform.

`ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true make live-apply-service service=runtime-ai-pool env=production EXTRA_ARGS='-e bypass_promotion=true'` completed successfully from the refreshed exact-main worktree with final recap `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=269 changed=144 unreachable=0 failed=0`, `monitoring-lv3 : ok=38 changed=0 unreachable=0 failed=0`, `proxmox_florin : ok=41 changed=8 unreachable=0 failed=0`, and `runtime-ai-lv3 : ok=305 changed=4 unreachable=0 failed=0`.

verification.check: Exact-main runtime-ai substrate, retirement, and gateway verification
verification.observed: Post-verify checks confirmed the private Traefik ping endpoint, Dapr metadata endpoint, Apache Tika version endpoint, Gotenberg health endpoint, Tesseract OCR health endpoint, Dapr-to-Tika invocation bridge, active `lv3-nomad` service on `runtime-ai-lv3`, healthy `runtime-ai` namespace visibility from `monitoring-lv3`, present `DOCKER` and `DOCKER-FORWARD` chains on the monitoring guest, healthy `dozzle-agent`, retired legacy document-extraction containers on `docker-runtime-lv3`, and a healthy authenticated `https://api.lv3.org/v1/gotenberg/health` response.

verification.check: Final repository automation and publish gates
verification.observed: The final merged `0.177.142` integration tree passed the closing local repository validation bundle (`data-models`, `workstream-surfaces`, `generated-docs`, `generated-portals`, and `agent-standards`), the refreshed remote validation lane, and the full pre-push gate including `ansible-lint`, `ansible-syntax`, generated artifacts, schema and policy validation, Semgrep SAST, security scan, integration tests, and packer/OpenTofu validation.

notes: This receipt is the canonical exact-main record for ADR 0319 and ADR 0320. The earlier branch-local runtime-ai receipts remain historical evidence for the first replay and the troubleshooting cycle that produced the bounded monitoring-only Docker bridge-chain recovery path.

The initial exact-main release surfaces were written successfully for repository version `0.177.141`; the only release-manager failure was the external post-write Outline sync to `wiki.lv3.org`, which returned `502 Bad Gateway`.

After ADR 0316 occupied repository version `0.177.141` on `origin/main`, the final merge-to-main integration promoted this verified runtime-ai rollout as repository version `0.177.142` while keeping platform version `0.130.89` unchanged.
The final merged `0.177.142` tree passed the closing local validation bundle, the remote validation lane, and the full pre-push gate before publication to `origin/main`.
